what_it_does: This file is a generated surface specification for view D1 in journey J3; it records the view's identity, platforms, represented intent, status, associated journey references, a list of UI-scheme addresses, and build targets for components, lint, and tokens.
observations: The title is 'Trigger a build (build-intent entry)'.
Home journey is J3 and journeys contains only J3.
addresses are UI-scheme URIs spanning canvas, chat, and inbox contexts.
build_to names components canvas, chat, inbox and includes a design_lint command plus a tokens mapping.
The file declares it was built by a Claude Code session and says addresses are never invented.
standouts: Build-intent entry title paired with WIRE-intent representation.
build_to block combines components, a lint command, and a token source path.
Addresses mix canvas wiring, chat wiring, and inbox build-intent/review/context nodes.
conventions: Follows a JSON blueprint format with id, area, title, platforms, status, journeys, and addresses.
Uses UI-scheme URIs as addressable node references.
Includes a generated-metadata '_what' field explaining provenance.
concerns: Status is 'planned', so the spec is not yet implemented.
No cross_journeys even though addresses touch canvas, chat, and inbox.
design_lint target is 'canvas/app/src' while build_to components include chat and inbox.
The tokens mapping uses a literal arrow character (→) inside a JSON string.
notes: A planned blueprint surface for the D1 build-intent entry in journey J3, wiring canvas, chat, and inbox UI addresses and declaring build outputs.
questions: What exactly does 'WIRE-intent' denote in this system?
How are the listed UI addresses validated to ensure they are not invented?
What is the 'B3' web referenced in the _what description?
Should the design_lint target cover chat and inbox as well as canvas?
Why is cross_journeys empty when the addresses span three components?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it records a generated surface spec for D1 with address references and build targets.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a concrete, addressable UI surface for initiating a build, linking the relevant canvas, chat, and inbox nodes and specifying what the build should produce.
novelty: It uses UI-scheme address URIs as first-class references and a build_to block that couples components with a lint command and a design-token source path.
contribution: It contributes a journey-local surface specification that maps a build-intent view to a set of UI addressable nodes and declares downstream build artifacts, supporting traceability from design intent to build outputs.
summary_for_embedding: A planned JSON surface specification for view D1 in journey J3 titled 'Trigger a build (build-intent entry)', listing desktop/mobile platforms, WIRE-intent representation, UI addresses across canvas, chat, and inbox, and build targets for components, lint, and design tokens.
intention_for_embedding: To record the addressable UI surface and build artifacts for the build-intent entry point in journey J3.
